forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+project: claude-model-select
+title: 'fix(review): template-context default model="claude" defeats the claude-code-haiku review fallback'
+---
+## Symptom
+`sah` review tool should default to `claude-code-haiku` (spawn `claude --model haiku`) on an unconfigured project, but it actually runs plain `claude` (no `--model`). qwen/llama review works; the Claude haiku tier silently does not.
+
+## Root cause (verified end-to-end)
+The spawn seam and the `ModelManager` resolver family are BOTH correct and already deliver `["--model","haiku"]` when handed the `claude-code-haiku` ModelConfig. The bug is upstream, in how the serve wiring picks the review model NAME.
+
+`apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/serve/mod.rs::review_model_config` resolves the name from the **template context**, not the config files:
+```
+let review_model  = cli_context.template_context.get("review.model")...;  // None when unset
+let default_model = cli_context.template_context.get("model")...;          // <-- NOT None in prod
+let model_name = ModelManager::review_agent_name_from(review_model, default_model);
+```
+`review_agent_name_from(a,b) = a.or(b).unwrap_or(REVIEW_DEFAULT_AGENT)`, so the `claude-code-haiku` default is only reached when BOTH inputs are `None`.
+
+But in production the top-level `model` template var is **never None**: `TemplateContext::set_default_variables()` â†’ `set_model_variable()` injects the literal `"claude"` (`resolve_model_name()` returns `"claude"`, template_context.rs ~666/671) whenever `model` is unset. Production builds this context via `main.rs` â†’ `load_cli_configuration()` â†’ `TemplateContext::load_for_cli()` (which calls `set_default_variables()`), then hands it to `CliContext`.
+
+So for an unconfigured project the serve path computes:
+- `default_model = Some("claude")` â†’ `model_name = "claude"` (NOT `claude-code-haiku`).
+- `find_agent_by_name("claude")` â†’ NotFound (only `claude-code` and `claude-code-haiku` builtins exist) â†’ `review_model_config` returns `None` (logs "review model 'claude' could not be resolved").
+- `wire_review_factories` then falls back to the global `tool_context.agent_config` = `ModelConfig::claude_code()` = plain `claude-code`, **no `--model`**.
+
+Net: review runs full Claude, never haiku. The `{{ model }}` Liquid prompt default ("claude") is leaking into agent/model SELECTION.
+
+## Why the existing test didn't catch it (test masks prod)
+`commands::serve::tests::test_review_model_config_defaults_to_haiku_when_unset` is GREEN but builds its CliContext from a raw `TemplateContext::new()` (helper `cli_context_with_models`) and **never calls `set_default_variables()`**. So in-test `get("model")` is `None` â†’ resolves to haiku. The fixture diverges from production, which always runs `set_default_variables()`. Classic fixture-only masking (see project memory `real-path-tests-not-mocks`, `fixture-only-anti-pattern`).
+
+## Fix (minimal, correct)
+Make review model selection read CONFIG FILES via the canonical resolver, not the prompt-rendering template vars:
+- In `review_model_config` (serve/mod.rs), resolve the name via `ModelManager::resolve_review_agent_name(&ModelPaths::sah())` and the config via `ModelManager::resolve_review_agent_config(&ModelPaths::sah())` (these read `review.model` / top-level `model:` from the `.sah` config through `get_review_agent`/`get_agent`, which correctly return `None` when unset â†’ fall through to `claude-code-haiku`). Stop reading `template_context.get("model")` / `get("review.model")` for selection entirely.
+- Keep the existing decision-point `tracing::info!("review scope â†’ â€¦")` log.
+- Document `resolve_model_name`/`set_model_variable` as PROMPT-RENDERING ONLY â€” never agent selection.
+
+This is the correct subset of sibling task `f7wcj2r`'s item #2. NOTE: `f7wcj2r` mislabels its item #1 (the `server.rs:610` `template_context.get_agent_config(None)` error-branch fallback for the GLOBAL agent) as "the actual bug" â€” it is NOT: that branch only fires on a resolver ERROR and feeds the global default, and fixing it alone leaves review still non-haiku. The real trigger is `review_model_config` consuming the defaulted `model="claude"` template var. The `server.rs:610` cleanup and the broader `get_agent_config` removal are reasonable hygiene but are NOT the review-haiku root cause and should not be conflated with it.
+
+## Tests (must reproduce production)
+- [ ] Rewrite the serve `review_model_config` tests so the fixture goes through `set_default_variables()` (or `load_for_cli`) â€” i.e. the same context shape production uses â€” so an UNSET review/overall model still resolves to `claude-code-haiku` (`--model haiku`). The current green test must be made to actually exercise the defaulted `model="claude"` var.
+- [ ] Regression: setting the template `model` var to a bogus value MUST NOT change the resolved review ModelConfig (proves template vars are not consulted for selection).
+- [ ] Keep the existing real-path spawn-seam assertion (`extra_args == ["--model","haiku"]`) green.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli -p swissarmyhammer-config` + `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` green.
+
+## Observability already in place
+Working tree has an uncommitted argv log in `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs::execute_spawn` ("â–¶ claude-agent process start: â€¦") â€” keep it; it is how an operator proves `--model haiku` (or its absence) on a real review run.
+
+## Workflow
+Use `/tdd`: first make the serve test reproduce production (RED â€” it should resolve to "claude"/None, not haiku), then apply the resolver fix (GREEN). #haiku
\ No newline at end of file

-`sah` review tool should default to `claude-code-haiku` (spawn `claude --model haiku`) on an unconfigured project, but it actually runs plain `claude` (no `--model`). qwen/llama review works; the Claude haiku tier silently does not.

-```
-let review_model  = cli_context.template_context.get("review.model")...;  // None when unset
-let default_model = cli_context.template_context.get("model")...;          // <-- NOT None in prod

-
-So for an unconfigured project the serve path computes:

-- `find_agent_by_name("claude")` â†’ NotFound (only `claude-code` and `claude-code-haiku` builtins exist) â†’ `review_model_config` returns `None` (logs "review model 'claude' could not be resolved").
-- `wire_review_factories` then falls back to the global `tool_context.agent_config` = `ModelConfig::claude_code()` = plain `claude-code`, **no `--model`**.
-
-Net: review runs full Claude, never haiku. The `{{ model }}` Liquid prompt default ("claude") is leaking into agent/model SELECTION.

-## Why the existing test didn't catch it (test masks prod)
-`commands::serve::tests::test_review_model_config_defaults_to_haiku_when_unset` is GREEN but builds its CliContext from a raw `TemplateContext::new()` (helper `cli_context_with_models`) and **never calls `set_default_variables()`**. -## Fix (minimal, correct)
-Make review model selection read CONFIG FILES via the canonical resolver, not the prompt-rendering template vars:
-- In `review_model_config` (serve/mod.rs), resolve the name via `ModelManager::resolve_review_agent_name(&ModelPaths::sah())` and the config via `ModelManager::resolve_review_agent_config(&ModelPaths::sah())` (these read `review.model` / top-level `model:` from the `.sah` config through `get_review_agent`/`get_agent`, which correctly return `None` when unset â†’ fall through to `claude-code-haiku`). Stop reading `template_context.get("model")` / `get("review.model")` for selection entirely.
-- Keep the existing decision-point `tracing::info!("review scope â†’ â€¦")` log.
-- Document `resolve_model_name`/`set_model_variable` as PROMPT-RENDERING ONLY â€” never agent selection.
-
-This is the correct subset of sibling task `f7wcj2r`'s item #2. NOTE: `f7wcj2r` mislabels its item #1 (the `server.rs:610` `template_context.get_agent_config(None)` error-branch fallback for the GLOBAL agent) as "the actual bug" â€” it is NOT: that branch only fires on a resolver ERROR and feeds the global default, and fixing it alone leaves review still non-haiku. forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -50,4 +50,13 @@
 Working tree has an uncommitted argv log in `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs::execute_spawn` ("â–¶ claude-agent process start: â€¦") â€” keep it; it is how an operator proves `--model haiku` (or its absence) on a real review run.

 ## Workflow
-Use `/tdd`: first make the serve test reproduce production (RED â€” it should resolve to "claude"/None, not haiku), then apply the resolver fix (GREEN). #haiku
\ No newline at end of file
+Use `/tdd`: first make the serve test reproduce production (RED â€” it should resolve to "claude"/None, not haiku), then apply the resolver fix (GREEN). #haiku
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-15 09:30)
+
+Scope: working-tree changes vs HEAD in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/serve/mod.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-config/src/template_context.rs`, `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs`. Verified: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli --lib review_model_config` (8 passed) and `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-cli -p swissarmyhammer-config --all-targets -- -D warnings` (clean).
+
+The fix is correct and matches the task intent. `review_model_config` now resolves the review model name via `ModelManager::resolve_review_agent_name(&ModelPaths::sah())` and no longer consults `template_context.get("model")`/`get("review.model")` for selection; an unconfigured scope falls through `get_review_agent`=None + `get_agent`=None â†’ `review_agent_name_from` â†’ `claude-code-haiku`. The `Option<Arc<ModelConfig>>` return reconciles cleanly with `wire_review_factories`' `unwrap_or_else(global)`, and the doc comment correctly justifies parsing the name locally (rather than calling `resolve_review_agent_config`) so the misconfigured-name â†’ server-live-`agent_config` fallback is preserved rather than re-resolving to the config-file global. The rewritten tests build a production-shaped `CliContext` (`set_default_variables()` injects `model="claude"`) and express configured scenarios by writing real `.sah` config, and `test_review_model_config_ignores_template_model_var` is a genuine, non-tautological regression guard.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/claude-agent/src/claude_process.rs:282-300` â€” The new argv-logging block in `execute_spawn` re-derives the same `program + redact_system_prompt_value(args) + .join(" ")` argv string that `log_command` already builds and emits via `tracing::info!` two lines earlier (`log_command` is called at ~467, `execute_spawn` at ~469 on the same `command`). It is therefore a near-verbatim third copy of an existing log, differing only by the added `session_id` field and the "â–¶ â€¦ process start" wording. This is the task-sanctioned, deliberately-preserved instrumentation, so leaving it is acceptable; if the duplication is ever consolidated, fold the `session_id` into `log_command` and drop the `execute_spawn` block so there is a single argv-logging code path. No action required for this task.
\ No newline at end of file

The `Option<Arc<ModelConfig>>` return reconciles cleanly with `wire_review_factories`' `unwrap_or_else(global)`, and the doc comment correctly justifies parsing the name locally (rather than calling `resolve_review_agent_config`) so the misconfigured-name â†’ server-live-`agent_config` fallback is preserved rather than re-resolving to the config-file global.  ## Observability already in place
